title: hieracium mexicanum subsp. niveopappum
intro: Hieracium mexicanum subsp. niveopappum, also known as Mexican hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family, characterized by its white or pale yellow flowers and feathery foliage. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Hieracium mexicanum subsp. niveopappum include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate skin irritations, making it a valuable herbal remedy. Herbal preparations of the plant often involve drying the leaves and flowers, then infusing them in hot water or oil to create teas, tinctures, and topical creams, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
